title: malus fusca
intro: Malus fusca, also known as the dark-leaved crabapple or Oregon crabapple, is a species of deciduous shrub or small tree in the family Rosaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Malus fusca have been shown to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Malus fusca can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support digestive health, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues.
